sentence: Subjective:
HPI: Judy Gomez, a 61-year-old female, presents for ongoing management of psoriatic arthritis.
She reports doing well, having decreased her prednisone dose to one tablet per day, which she finds sufficient.
She mentions occasional increased pain in her feet, particularly on days off from her desk job when she is more active.
She reports taking an extra prednisone tablet once a week for these periods of increased pain.
She also mentions that the methotrexate has helped her joint pain.
She expresses concern about bruising on her skin, which she attributes to the prednisone, but notes that she does not feel the bruising when it occurs.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of psoriatic arthritis, for which she is currently on prednisone and methotrexate.
She has had COVID-19 vaccination and took methotrexate the week after the first dose, which resulted in significant side effects.
Review of systems: The patient reports no additional symptoms in other organs that are relevant to her initial presentation.
Current medications: Prednisone 1 tablet per day, methotrexate (dose and frequency not specified), and COVID-19 vaccine (status not specified).
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient has a good range of motion in her toes.
She has ridges on her nails, which are consistent with psoriasis.
There is tenderness in her joint areas, particularly in her feet and knees.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has psoriatic arthritis, which is well-managed with methotrexate and prednisone, but she is at risk for increased bruising due to the prednisone.
Problem list: Psoriatic arthritis; prednisone-induced bruising; suspected methotrexate-vaccine interaction.
Plan:
I will discontinue prednisone and continue with methotrexate.
I will instruct the patient to schedule an appointment for blood work at the front desk.
I will see the patient in three months to reevaluate her treatment plan.
Follow-up: The patient will return in three months for a follow-up appointment.
